title: California Consumer Privacy Act (CCPA) Signed
description: California Governor Jerry Brown signed the CCPA (AB 375) into law, making California the first US state with a comprehensive consumer privacy law.

title: CCPA Takes Effect
description: The California Consumer Privacy Act became enforceable, granting California residents rights to know, delete, and opt out of sale of personal information.

title: Virginia CDPA Signed
description: Virginia became the second US state to enact a comprehensive privacy law with the Consumer Data Protection Act.

title: Colorado Privacy Act Signed
description: Colorado became the third state with comprehensive privacy legislation.

title: Utah Consumer Privacy Act Signed
description: Utah became the fourth state with comprehensive privacy law, notable for its more business-friendly approach.

title: Connecticut SB 6 Signed
description: Connecticut enacted its comprehensive Data Privacy Act.

title: CPRA Takes Effect (California)
description: The California Privacy Rights Act expanded CCPA with new rights and established the California Privacy Protection Agency.

title: Multiple State Laws Take Effect
description: Colorado Privacy Act, Connecticut Data Privacy Act, and Virginia CDPA enforcement all began on this date.

title: Montana, Oregon, and Texas Privacy Laws Take Effect
description: Three more states began enforcing comprehensive privacy legislation.

title: Tennessee TIPA Takes Effect
description: Tennessee Information Protection Act became enforceable.

title: Delaware, Iowa, New Hampshire, New Jersey Privacy Laws Take Effect
description: Four additional states began comprehensive privacy enforcement.

title: Indiana, Kentucky, Rhode Island Privacy Laws Take Effect
description: Three more states join the comprehensive privacy law landscape.
